events.text: Patient is a 64-year-old African American male, height 6 feet 0 inches, weight 220 pounds, ECOG performance status 1.

At age 64, patient presented with 4 months of progressive bone pain in the ribs and pelvis, unintentional weight loss of 15 pounds, and fatigue; workup revealed hemoglobin 10.5 g/dL, creatinine 1.2 mg/dL, calcium 10.9 mg/dL, beta-2 microglobulin 4.0 mg/L, albumin 3.8 g/dL consistent with ISS Stage II multiple myeloma.

At age 64, bone marrow biopsy from site C42.1 showed 42% plasma cells with histology consistent with multiple myeloma (ICD-O morphology 9732/3); immunohistochemistry positive for CD138 and cytoplasmic IgG lambda; CD56 positive, CD20 negative; amyloid stain negative.

At age 64, FISH analysis on bone marrow aspirate showed standard-risk profile with hyperdiploidy; no del(17p), t(4;14), t(14;16), or gain(1q21) detected; R-ISS Stage II based on ISS II, normal LDH at 185 U/L, and standard-risk cytogenetics.

At age 64, serum protein electrophoresis showed M-spike of 2.8 g/dL in the gamma region; immunofixation confirmed IgG lambda monoclonal protein; serum free light chain lambda 155 mg/L, kappa 16 mg/L, kappa/lambda ratio 0.10; 24-hour urine M-protein 0.8 g/day.

At age 64, PET/CT showed multiple FDG-avid lytic lesions in the pelvis, bilateral ribs, and lumbar spine; largest lesion in the right iliac bone measuring 2.8 cm with SUV of 6.5; focal lesion in the left 7th rib with cortical thinning; no extramedullary disease.

At age 64, initiated VRd induction therapy with standard dosing on 28-day cycles; started zoledronic acid 4 mg IV monthly for bone protection, acyclovir for varicella prophylaxis, and aspirin for DVT prophylaxis.
At age 64, cycles 1 and 2 of VRd completed; M-spike decreased from 2.8 to 1.4 g/dL; serum free lambda light chain decreased to 72 mg/L; tolerated well with grade 1 fatigue only.

At age 64, cycles 3 and 4 of VRd completed; post-cycle 4 SPEP showed M-spike 0.4 g/dL; serum free lambda light chain 25 mg/L with kappa/lambda ratio 0.64 normalizing; response classified as very good partial response.
At age 64, after cycle 4, patient developed grade 1 peripheral neuropathy in toes bilaterally; bortezomib continued at full dose with close monitoring.

At age 64, completed 2 additional cycles of VRd for total of 6; post-induction SPEP showed no M-spike; immunofixation showed trace IgG lambda band; classified as very good partial response bordering on complete response.

At age 65, underwent stem cell mobilization with G-CSF plus plerixafor; collected 6.5 million CD34-positive cells per kilogram; received high-dose melphalan 200 mg/m2 followed by autologous stem cell transplant.
At age 65, post-transplant complicated by grade 2 mucositis and grade 2 nausea; no febrile neutropenia; engraftment on day plus 12; discharged day plus 15 in good condition.

At age 65, day plus 100 bone marrow biopsy showed less than 2% plasma cells with no aberrant phenotype on flow cytometry; SPEP no M-spike; immunofixation negative; serum free light chain ratio normalized; MRD by multiparameter flow cytometry at 10^-5 sensitivity was negative; classified as stringent complete response with MRD negativity.
At age 65, started lenalidomide maintenance at 10 mg days 1 through 21 of 28-day cycle; tolerated well.
At age 66, annual PET/CT showed resolution of all previously FDG-avid lesions; healing sclerotic changes in previously lytic areas; no new osseous or extramedullary disease.
At age 66, one year post-transplant; maintained in stringent complete response with MRD negativity; all labs normal; ECOG performance status 0; lenalidomide maintenance continued.

At age 67, two years post-transplant; sustained stringent complete response; developed grade 1 muscle cramps attributed to lenalidomide; managed with magnesium supplementation; lenalidomide continued at 10 mg.

At age 68, four years post-transplant; developed second primary malignancy with biopsy-proven basal cell carcinoma on left forearm; excised with clear margins; attributed to possible lenalidomide-associated increased risk; lenalidomide maintenance continued after multidisciplinary discussion.

At age 68, continued in sustained stringent complete response; MRD negative on repeat bone marrow biopsy at 10^-5 sensitivity; six years from diagnosis with excellent disease control on lenalidomide maintenance; annual dermatologic screening recommended.

scenario_blurb: Multiple myeloma, ISS stage II-III. IgG kappa or IgA lambda subtypes. Treated with VRd (bortezomib, lenalidomide, dexamethasone) induction followed by autologous stem cell transplant and lenalidomide maintenance. Include serum protein electrophoresis, free light chains, beta-2 microglobulin, FISH for del(17p) and t(4;14).
